company.name: Viridian Therapeutics
company.subtitle: Post-REVEAL-1 · Batoclimab TED failure (Apr 2 2026) clears competitive path

us.patientsK.note: AAO/Graves' Foundation: ~50K US Thyroid Eye Disease (Tepezza-addressable moderate-severe active).

eu.patientsK.note: EUGOGO: ~70K EU-5 TED moderate-severe; Tepezza EMA approved 2025; historically IV steroids + orbital radiation.

row.patientsK.note: Global TED ~500K; ex-US/EU ~30K accessible in specialty endocrine/ophthalmology centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Tepezza (Amgen/Horizon) first-in-class; strong proptosis reduction data; PA + pricing scrutiny.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- EMA approved 2025; HTA ongoing; competitive IV steroid standard still cheap.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan Amgen partnership; rest ROW limited access.

us.priceK.note: Tepezza ~$450K per 8-infusion course (Q3W x 8); typically 1 course, retreatment rare.

penPct.note: ~30% peak of moderate-severe active TED; competition from Viridian VRDN-003 SC late-stage.

us.reachPct.note: Veligrotug IGF-1R IV P3 REVEAL-1/-2 readout 2026; 25% of ~15-20K active US TED pts if approved late 2026 vs Tepezza ($460K) switch opportunity.

us.priceK.note: $340K/course WAC modeled (5 infusions q3wk); 25% discount to Tepezza to drive switching.

us.wtpPct.note: 65% -- payors require CAS>=3, proptosis, diplopia; hearing-loss monitoring required; step-edit vs Tepezza reduced if non-inferior.

eu.reachPct.note: 15% of ~20K EU TED; EMA filing 2027 (Tepezza not approved EU, opportunity).

eu.wtpPct.note: 60% -- no incumbent IGF-1R approved EU, clear path.

us.patientsK.note: AAO/Graves' Foundation: ~50K US Thyroid Eye Disease (Tepezza-addressable moderate-severe active).

eu.patientsK.note: EUGOGO: ~70K EU-5 TED moderate-severe; Tepezza EMA approved 2025; historically IV steroids + orbital radiation.

row.patientsK.note: Global TED ~500K; ex-US/EU ~30K accessible in specialty endocrine/ophthalmology centers.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- Tepezza (Amgen/Horizon) first-in-class; strong proptosis reduction data; PA + pricing scrutiny.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- EMA approved 2025; HTA ongoing; competitive IV steroid standard still cheap.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan Amgen partnership; rest ROW limited access.

us.priceK.note: Tepezza ~$450K per 8-infusion course (Q3W x 8); typically 1 course, retreatment rare.

penPct.note: ~30% peak of moderate-severe active TED; competition from Viridian VRDN-003 SC late-stage.

us.reachPct.note: Elegrobart (VRDN-003) SC IGF-1R P3 readout 2027; 35% of US TED given SC convenience vs IV; could capture naive and Tepezza-experienced.

us.priceK.note: $320K/course WAC modeled; SC auto-injector premium vs IV but lower admin cost.

us.wtpPct.note: 70% -- SC route reduces infusion-center burden; payors favor on convenience + cost; PA similar criteria to IV.

eu.reachPct.note: 25% of EU TED; SC form preferred EU given infusion infrastructure.

mega_bear.narrative: Veli CRL + ele fails — argenx takes TED
events: ✗ FDA issues CRL for veligrotug — manufacturing or safety concern
✗ argenx efgartigimod Phase 3 in TED succeeds — FcRn not dead after all
✗ Elegrobart REVEAL-2 fails to replicate
→ No approved product, franchise collapses to cash floor

bear.narrative: Veli approved but Tepezza dominates + ele SC underwhelms
events: ✓ Veli approved but slow uptake
✗ Tepezza entrenched, price war
✗ Ele SC niche too small
→ Single-product, limited share gain

base.narrative: Veli best-in-class IV + ele SC expands access — FcRn TED path dead
events: ✓ Veli approved, takes share from Tepezza
✓ Batoclimab failure removes key FcRn competitor
✓ Ele SC Phase 3 positive — convenience advantage
→ Only IGF-1R franchise challenging Tepezza

bull.narrative: TED franchise dominance — only real alternative to Tepezza, M&A target
events: ✓ Veli takes majority IV share from Tepezza
✓ Ele SC expands total TED market (chronic + convenience)
✓ FcRn path dead in TED — IGF-1R validated as best approach
✓ M&A target (only pure-play TED franchise)

psychedelic_bull.narrative: IGF-1R franchise monopoly in TED + Graves' expansion — Amgen acquires
events: ✓ Veli approved Jun 2026 — rapid Tepezza share capture
✓ Ele SC approved — only SC option for TED, expands chronic market
✓ Viridian expands IGF-1R into Graves' disease (massive TAM)
✓ Amgen/Horizon acquires Viridian to own entire TED franchise

lastData.title: REVEAL-1 elegrobart Ph3
points: Apr 2: Immunovant batoclimab FAILED both Ph3 TED trials — FcRn approach dead in TED

risks: Tepezza still entrenched with first-mover advantage
argenx efgartigimod Phase 3 in TED still ongoing (FcRn not fully dead)
Cannibalization between veli IV and ele SC
